title: [FIRRTL] (xor) mismatch
body:
The following FIRRTL program

```python
; seed: 2216
circuit top_mod :
  module top_mod :
    input inp_j: UInt<3>
    output _tmp10: UInt<4>
    _tmp10 <= xor(asSInt(inp_j), SInt<4>("h0"))
```

Compiled with `firtool --lower-to-rtl --lower-types` produces this Verilog:

```verilog
// Standard header to adapt well known macros to our needs.

module top_mod(
  input  [2:0] inp_j,
  output [3:0] _tmp10);

  assign _tmp10 = {inp_j[2], inp_j};	// a_top_mod.fir:3:3, :6:15
endmodule
```

Compiled with `firrtl-1.5-SNAPSHOT --dont-fold div` produces this Verilog:

```verilog
module top_mod(
  input  [2:0] inp_j,
  output [3:0] _tmp10
);
  assign _tmp10 = inp_j;
endmodule
```

`Yosys 0.9+3911 (git sha1 4b31223e, clang 11.0.1 -fPIC -Os)` reports formal mismatch:

```
ERROR: Found 1 unproven $equiv cells in 'equiv_status -assert'.
```
